title: 10 best herbal essential oils for gastritis
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for gastritis are natural remedies derived from plants that help alleviate the inflammation and discomfort associated with gastritis. These oils can reduce inflammation, soothe the digestive tract, and promote healing. The best herbal essential oils for gastritis include Zingiber officinale, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Foeniculum vulgare, Curcuma longa, Mentha x piperita, Cinchona officinalis, Silybum marianum, Cinnamomum verum, Ginkgo biloba, and Aloe barbadensis. If left untreated, gastritis can lead to chronic abdominal pain, nausea, vomiting, and digestive problems, severely impacting daily life and overall well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint essential oils helps with gastritis because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation in the stomach lining. The menthol content in peppermint oil also relaxes the muscles in the digestive tract, alleviating spasms and cramps often associated with gastritis. Additionally, peppermint oil's cooling sensation can soothe the stomach and provide relief from heartburn and indigestion. As a result, peppermint essential oils have been traditionally used to calm and protect the stomach, providing natural relief from gastritis symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic: Peppermint essential oil helps to relax and reduce spasms in the digestive tract, providing relief from abdominal pain and cramping associated with gastritis.
Anti-inflammatory: The oil's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, which can exacerbate gastritis symptoms such as bloating, nausea, and vomiting.

Digestive stimulant: Peppermint essential oil stimulates digestive enzymes and improves gut motility, helping to alleviate symptoms of gastroparesis and reducing the risk of gastric acid reflux that can contribute to gastritis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in menthol, peppermint roots have natural anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe digestive issues and alleviate symptoms of gastritis.
Barks: The bark of the peppermint plant contains high amounts of rosmarinic acid, which has been shown to reduce inflammation and ease digestive discomfort associated with gastritis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves at dawn and dry them in the shade for several hours.
Combine one part dried peppermint leaves with three parts carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil.
Steep the mixture in a warm dark place for 24 to 48 hours allowing the essential oils to infuse.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth and discard the solids then store the infused oil in a cool dark place.
Transfer the infused oil to a clean glass bottle and label it with the preparation date and storage instructions.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice essential oils helps with gastritis because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe the stomach lining and reduce inflammation. The oil's natural compounds, such as glycyrrhizin and licoricidin, have been shown to calm the digestive system, alleviate symptoms like heartburn and bloating, and promote healing of the mucous membrane. Additionally, herbal licorice essential oils may also help to stimulate digestion, improve appetite, and reduce nausea, providing relief from gastritis-related discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Licorice oil's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce inflammation in the stomach and digestive tract, alleviating symptoms such as pain, bloating, and discomfort associated with gastritis.

Antacid property: The alkaloids present in licorice oil neutralize stomach acid and help to buffer the pH of the digestive tract, providing relief from heartburn, indigestion, and other gastrointestinal issues.

Stomach-soothing property: Licorice oil's flavonoids and saponins have a soothing effect on the mucous membranes lining the stomach, reducing irritation and inflammation, and promoting healing of gastric ulcers and erosions.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant have been traditionally used in traditional medicine due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them ideal for treating gastritis symptoms.

Barks: The barks of the licorice plant contain flavonoids and saponins that have anti-inflammatory and soothing effects, which can help alleviate gastritis symptoms such as inflammation and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots in the early morning or evening when moisture content is lowest.
Clean and wash the roots thoroughly with distilled water to remove impurities and dirt.
Dry the roots using a food dehydrator at 60°c for 24 hours to reduce moisture content to below 10%.
Steam distill the dried roots in a double-bottomed flask equipped with a condenser using distilled water and collecting essential oil as it separates from aqueous fraction within 2-3 hours.
Filter the resulting essential oil through a coffee filter paper into dark glass bottles stored in a cool dry place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger essential oils helps with gastritis because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and discomfort in the stomach lining. The oil's active compound, gingerol, has been shown to inhibit the production of prostaglandins, which are hormones that can cause inflammation and pain in the digestive tract. Additionally, ginger essential oils have natural antacid properties, helping to neutralize stomach acid and alleviate symptoms such as heartburn and bloating associated with gastritis.
remedy_properties: Digestive Stimulant: Ginger essential oil helps to stimulate digestion and alleviate symptoms of gastritis by increasing salivary secretion and enhancing digestive enzyme activity.

Anti-Inflammatory: Herbal ginger essential oil has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, alleviating pain, discomfort, and nausea associated with gastritis.
Antispasmodic: Ginger essential oil helps to relax smooth muscle tissue in the stomach and intestines, reducing spasms and cramps that can exacerbate gastritis symptoms.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The thick, underground stems of the ginger plant, which contain active compounds like gingerols and shogaols that help alleviate stomach inflammation and pain associated with gastritis.
Roots: The main storage organ of the ginger plant, rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that can help soothe digestive issues and reduce symptoms of gastritis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginger roots carefully extracting only the outer layer.
Dry the extracted ginger layer in a warm ventilated area for seven days.
Grind the dried ginger into fine powder using a mortar and pestle grinder.
Steam distill 500 grams of the powdered ginger with 1000 milliliters water for three hours.
Filter the resulting oil through cheesecloth or coffee filters then store in dark glass bottles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass essential oils helps with gastritis because of its anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort. The oil's natural antibacterial qualities also help combat harmful bacteria that can exacerbate gastritis symptoms. Additionally, lemongrass essential oils have a calming effect on the stomach muscles, easing cramps and spasms associated with gastritis. Its refreshing citrus scent also promotes relaxation, which can aid in reducing stress-induced digestive issues.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Helps to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramps associated with gastritis.
Anti-inflammatory property: Reduces inflammation in the stomach lining, which can contribute to gastritis symptoms such as pain and discomfort.

Carminative property: Relieves gas and bloating by increasing the movement of gas through the digestive system and preventing its accumulation, which can alleviate gastritis symptoms like abdominal distension.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, lemongrass roots are excellent for calming digestive issues and reducing inflammation associated with gastritis.
Rhyzomes: The rhizome's anti-inflammatory properties help soothe stomach ulcers and reduce acidity levels in the body.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh lemongrass leaves at dawn on a sunny day for maximum potency.
Cleanse the harvested lemongrass by gently rinsing with distilled water and pat drying with paper towels.
Steam distill the cleaned lemongrass in a copper still using 500ml of water for 60 minutes to extract the essential oil.
Collect the extracted essential oil and dilute it with jojoba oil at a ratio of 1:2 (1 part oil to 2 parts carrier) for topical use.
Store the final product in dark glass bottles away from direct sunlight and heat, allowing it to mature for 24 hours before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric essential oils helps with gastritis because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation in the stomach lining. The curcumin present in turmeric has been shown to inhibit the production of prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that contribute to increased stomach acid secretion and inflammation. Additionally, turmeric's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can damage the stomach lining, promoting healing and reducing symptoms such as bloating, abdominal pain, and nausea associated with gastritis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Turmeric essential oil helps reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can alleviate symptoms such as bloating, cramping, and abdominal pain associated with gastritis.

Antioxidant property: The antioxidant properties of turmeric essential oil help neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and damage to the digestive lining, further exacerbating gastritis symptoms.

Wound-healing property: Turmeric essential oil promotes wound healing by stimulating the production of collagen and improving blood flow, which can aid in repairing damaged tissues in the stomach lining caused by gastritis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the turmeric plant contain a high concentration of curcumin, a potent anti-inflammatory compound that helps soothe and calm digestive issues associated with gastritis.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of turmeric have a rich content of volatile oils and sesquiterpenes, which help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of gastritis such as bloating and abdominal pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality turmeric roots ensure optimal potency and freshness.
Dry the roots in a warm dry place for at least two weeks to reduce moisture content.
Grind dried turmeric roots into fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 10 grams of powdered turmeric with 100 milliliters of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil.
Steep the mixture in a glass jar for three weeks shaking daily to extract essential compounds.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm essential oils helps with gastritis because of its soothing and calming properties that can ease digestive discomfort. remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality melissa officinalis leaves by hand early morning for optimal oil production.
Dry the harvested leaves using a food dehydrator at 35°c for 12 hours to preserve quality.
Steam distill the dried leaves using a 100 ml glass still and 50 ml of distilled water for 2 hours.
Monitor the distillation process and collect the essential oil when it reaches 1% concentration in the steam output.
Filter the collected essential oil through a coffee filter or cheesecloth to remove impurities before bottling and storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cumin essential oils helps with gastritis because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm the stomach lining. The oil's carminative and digestant compounds help reduce inflammation and ease digestion, alleviating symptoms such as bloating, cramping, and nausea. Additionally, cumin essential oil has antimicrobial properties that can help combat Helicobacter pylori bacteria, a common cause of gastritis, promoting a healthy gut environment and reducing the risk of complications.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh cuminum cyminum leaves and stems from organic sources.
Dry the harvested plant material using a warm air circulation oven at 35 degrees celsius for 12 hours.
Use a mechanical grinder or food processor to grind the dried cuminum cyminum into a fine powder with a particle size of less than one millimeter.
Add 500 grams of the ground cuminum cyminum to a high-proof vegetable oil such as sweet almond oil and leave it to infuse for 21 days in a dark glass container at room temperature.
Strain the infused mixture using cheesecloth or a coffee filter and transfer the resulting essential oil to dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel essential oils helps with gastritis because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm the digestive system. remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality foeniculum vulgare seeds free of impurities and store them in airtight containers.
Dry the harvested foeniculum vulgare seeds in a warm, well-ventilated area for 7-10 days to reduce moisture levels.
Steam distill the dried foeniculum vulgare seeds with 5 kg of water at 100°c for 4 hours using a still or extractor.
Monitor and record the essential oil yield and quality during the distillation process, aiming for a minimum of 15% essential oil concentration.
Bottle and label the extracted foeniculum vulgare essential oil in dark glass containers with tight-fitting lids, storing them at room temperature until use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower essential oils helps with gastritis because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The oil's active compounds, such as echinacoside and chicoric acid, have been shown to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms of gastritis like bloating, discomfort, and stomach pain. remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of fresh or dried echinacea angustifolia roots in the morning on a sunny day.
Dry the harvested roots in a well-ventilated area for at least 7 days before processing.
Chop 15 grams of dried echinacea angustifolia roots into small pieces using scissors or a mortar and pestle.
Combine the chopped roots with 150 milliliters of carrier oil, such as coconut or jojoba oil, in a glass jar.
Steep the mixture for at least 14 days in a cool dark place before straining and filtering the essential oil.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder essential oils helps with gastritis because of its anti-inflammatory and antacid properties. The oil's active compounds, such as alpha-terpinene and beta-caryophyllene, have been shown to reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, alleviating symptoms like heartburn and indigestion. remedy_recipe: Harvest 30 grams of fresh sambucus nigra leaves and stems, cutting them just before dawn when they are most potent.
Dry the harvested material in a warm, well-ventilated area for 7 days, stirring occasionally to ensure even drying.
Use a steam distillation apparatus with a still pot capacity of 1 liter, adding 30 grams of dried sambucus nigra and 500 milliliters of distilled water to the still pot.
Distill the mixture at 100 degrees celsius for 2 hours, collecting the resulting essential oil in a glass container lined with aluminum foil.
Allow the essential oil to mature for 24 hours before filtering it through a coffee filter or paper towel into dark-tinted glass bottles for storage and use.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat gastritis with herbal essential oils is by combining certain oils that have natural anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. Mix equal parts of ginger, peppermint, and fennel oils in a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil and apply topically to the abdomen to ease discomfort and reduce inflammation. Additionally, inhaling these oils through steam inhalation or using them in a diffuser can also help alleviate symptoms.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to gastritis that are treated with herbal essential oils are irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), peptic ulcers, and acid reflux. Certain essential oils like peppermint, chamomile, ginger, and fennel have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that help alleviate symptoms such as bloating, cramps, and inflammation in the digestive tract. These oils can be used topically or added to a warm bath for relief from discomfort and pain.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale essential oils helps with gastritis because it has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce inflammation in the digestive tract. Zingiberene: Acts as an anti-inflammatory agent, reducing pain and inflammation in the stomach, and also has a gastroprotective effect by preventing acid secretion and promoting stomach lining repair.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of gingerols and shogaols, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe gastritis.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh zingiber officinale roots with a sharp tool at dawn to preserve their potency.
Clean the harvested roots under cold running water to remove dirt and debris thoroughly.
Steam distill the cleaned roots in a laboratory setting with a distillation apparatus at 100 degrees celsius.
Collect the resulting distillate in a glass container and separate the essential oil from water using a separating funnel.
Filter the essential oil through a paper filter to remove any remaining impurities and contaminants naturally.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra essential oils helps with gastritis because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The oil contains compounds such as glycyrrhizin, which has been shown to protect the stomach lining from damage and reduce inflammation. Glycyrrhizin: This triterpenoid saponin compound has potent anti-ulcer and anti-inflammatory properties that help protect the stomach lining from acid damage and reduce inflammation.
Isoliquiritin: This isomeric flavonoid compound also has anti-inflammatory properties and is known to inhibit the production of stomach acid, which helps alleviate symptoms of gastritis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used to make essential oils for gastritis because they contain the highest concentration of glycyrrhizin, a compound with anti-inflammatory properties.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 roots of glycyrrhiza glabra with a sharp knife to minimize damage.
Steam distill the harvested roots for 2-3 hours at 100-110°c to release oils.
Collect the distilled oil and separate it from water using a separatory funnel.
Filter the distilled oil through a 5-micron filter paper to remove impurities and sediment.
Store the filtered oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids at 15-25°c.

remedies.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare essential oils helps with gastritis because of its anti-inflammatory and carminative properties. Cineole: Cineole is an oxide terpene that has a calming effect on the stomach and intestines, reducing spasms and inflammation associated with gastritis.

remedy_recipe: Harvest foeniculum vulgare seeds and leaves when in full bloom for maximum oil content and flavor.
Dry the harvested foeniculum vulgare parts in a warm oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Steam distill the dried foeniculum vulgare parts at 2 bars pressure for 3 hours to obtain oil.
Filter the obtained oil through a coffee filter paper to remove impurities and sediment.

Flowers: The flowers of Silybum marianum are used to make essential oils, which contain flavonoids and other compounds that help reduce inflammation and soothe the stomach lining, providing relief from gastritis symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-15 kg of fresh silybum marianum flowers and leaves on a dry sunny day after morning dew has evaporated.
Steam distill the harvested plant material at 100-150°c for 2-3 hours to release the essential oil.
Collect the essential oil from the distillation process and separate it from the water using a condenser.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter to remove any impurities and improve its clarity.
Store the final herbal silybum marianum essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at room temperature.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinnamomum verum essential oils helps with gastritis because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The oil contains compounds like cinnamaldehyde, which has been shown to reduce inflammation in the stomach lining and improve digestion. Its antimicrobial properties help combat the growth of harmful bacteria and fungi that can contribute to gastritis. Additionally, cinnamomum verum essential oils have been found to soothe the stomach and reduce pain, making it a natural remedy for those suffering from gastritis.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: Acts as an anti-inflammatory agent to reduce inflammation and irritation in the stomach lining, thereby alleviating gastritis symptoms.
Cinnamaldehyde: Exhibits antimicrobial properties to inhibit the growth of harmful bacteria that can cause gastritis, promoting a healthy gut environment.
Eugenol: Demonstrates antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects to protect the stomach lining from damage, reduce inflammation, and promote healing in gastritis.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and protect the stomach lining.
Rhyzomes: Cinnamon oil, extracted from the inner bark of the rhyzome, has natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties that aid in soothing gastritis.
Buds: The buds of Cinnamomum verum contain oils that help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with gastritis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest mature cinnamomum verum leaves and twigs from the tree, taking care not to damage the plant.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm place with good air circulation for 7 to 10 days.
Steam distill the dried plant material using a still, collecting the steam for 2 to 3 hours.
Separate the essential oil from the water using a separatory funnel, collecting the oil at the bottom.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter or paper filter to remove any impurities, resulting in a clear oil.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba essential oils helps with gastritis because it has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm the stomach lining, reducing inflammation and irritation. The oil's antioxidant properties also help to neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate gastritis symptoms. Additionally, ginkgo biloba essential oil has been shown to enhance blood flow to the stomach, promoting healing and reducing pain associated with gastritis. Its ability to calm the stomach also promotes digestion and reduces nausea and discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Help with gastritis by reducing inflammation and preventing the breakdown of the stomach lining, thereby alleviating symptoms of gastritis.
Terpenoids: Help with gastritis by exhibiting antioxidant properties, which protect the stomach from oxidative stress and promote healing of the stomach lining.
Bilobalide: Help with gastritis by having anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of gastritis in the stomach.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part due to their rich content of flavonoids and terpenoids, which are believed to help alleviate gastritis symptoms.
Seeds: The seeds are another key component used to make essential oils for gastritis, as they contain ginkgotoxin, which may have anti-inflammatory properties.

Barks: The barks of the Ginkgo biloba tree are used to make essential oils due to their high content of terpenoids and flavonoids, which are thought to contribute to their potential benefits for gastritis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest ginkgo biloba leaves in late summer when they are fully mature and dry.
Steam distill the harvested leaves to extract the essential oil within 2 hours.
Collect the distillate and separate the essential oil from water by decantation within 30 minutes.
Filter the extracted essential oil through a paper filter or cotton gauze to remove impurities within 15 minutes.
Bottle the filtered essential oil in dark glass containers to preserve its potency and aroma immediately.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis essential oils helps with gastritis because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The oil contains compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin that have been shown to reduce inflammation and protect the mucous lining of the stomach. This can help alleviate symptoms of gastritis, such as heartburn, nausea, and abdominal pain. Additionally, aloe barbadensis oil may help promote the healing of the stomach lining, reducing irritation and discomfort associated with gastritis. This makes it a natural remedy for those seeking relief from gastritis.
remedy_constituents: Aloe-emodin: It has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with gastritis.
Anthraquinones: They have a protective effect on the gastric mucosa and can reduce the severity of gastritis by promoting the healing of gastric ulcers.
Catechins: These polyphenolic compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the stomach, alleviating symptoms of gastritis.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which help soothe and calm the digestive system.
Leaves: Contain aloe vera gel, which is high in mucilages that can protect and heal the mucous membranes in the stomach and intestines.
Leaves: The essential oil extracted from the leaves has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce inflammation and prevent oxidative stress in the digestive tract.
remedy_recipe: Harvest mature leaves with thick green skin and a vibrant green interior from aloe barbadensis plants.
Cut the harvested leaves into small pieces and soak them in water for 24 hours.
Transfer the soaked aloe leaves to a distillation apparatus and heat them to 100 degrees celsius.
Collect the resulting distillate and separate the essential oil from the water using a condenser.
Filter the essential oil through a paper filter to remove any impurities and store it in a dark glass bottle.
